data.title: SA Asks: What's the best silver play right now for investors?
data.description:
Silver prices reached an all-time high this past week amid increased industrial demand coupled with investors looking for safe haven investments.

We asked Seeking Alpha analysts Eliana Scialabba and Andrew Hecht what they view as the best silver play right now for investors.

Eliana Scialabba: I’ve been following silver closely through my recent coverage of Pan American Silver (NYSE:PAAS), Avino (NYSE:ASM) and Vizsla Silver (NYSE:VZLA), and I think this moment feels different. With silver above $53 per ounce this week, we’re not just seeing a monetary move but a more structural one. Industrial demand from solar, EVs, and electronics keeps rising while global supply stays tight, creating a setup that supports the whole sector. 

PAAS stands out as the large cash-flowing anchor, ASM captures mid-tier growth with discipline, and VZLA brings pure exploration torque. Even with their different sizes and stages, they’re all benefiting from the same broader shift. This small sample of companies shows why I believe it’s a good time not just for individual names but for the silver space as a whole, a sector finally being valued for both its scarcity and its strategic importance.

Andrew Hecht: In 1995, I ran the global precious metals trading and sales business at the Phibro division of Salomon Brothers, now Citigroup. I believed silver was inexpensive at between $5 and $6 per ounce. Together with three other senior traders, and with management's approval (and management was Warren Buffett), we purchased a proprietary silver position, investing over $1.25 billion in silver. We bought over 250 million ounces of the metal. I have been bullish on silver for decades, expecting the price to eventually rise above the 1980 $50.36 per ounce peak. 

Thirty years have passed since I participated in what likely remains the largest silver risk position in history, and the price has only now reached my target. However, the 1980 price of $50.36 adjusted for inflation is now just under $325 per ounce. I do not expect silver to reach that lofty level, but higher highs look likely. Even the most aggressive bull markets rarely move in straight lines, and silver is no exception. 

The most direct route for participating in the current silver bull market is the physical market for bars and coins. Futures are leveraged instruments, as margin requires a small down payment for controlling a significant risk position. The iShares Silver Trust ETF (NYSEARCA:SLV) purports to hold physical silver, but it owns allocated and unallocated silver, which could diverge from a value perspective. A diversified approach to silver mining stocks, utilizing Global X Silver Miners ETF (NYSEARCA:SIL) and Amplify Junior Silver Miners ETF (NYSEARCA:SILJ), offers an alternative investment and trading strategy, but senior and junior silver miners are not silver. 

Buying silver on price weakness has been optimal since the turn of this century. I have not changed my bullish view of the silver market, but caution against buying on rallies, which has always caused lots of indigestion. The $1.25 billion risk position in 1995 would be worth well over $12.5 billion today. The bottom line is that in markets, timing is everything!

  • Top Precious Metals and Minerals Stocks

data.title: Weekly Tech Check: AI strength boosts ON Semi and Micron; HPE drags on cautious outlook
data.description:
Technology stocks ended the week slightly lower as a mix of earnings updates, share offerings and financing news kept sentiment cautious. Companies across the sector announced new stock and warrant issuances, convertible note offerings and capital raises, while some AI-linked partnerships and product updates offered limited support.

The sector also saw continued regulatory and legal attention, adding to the cautious tone ahead of the earnings cycle. Several companies faced pressure from profit-margin concerns, valuation resets and selective downgrades.  A reported collaboration between OpenAI, Broadcom and Arm on new AI chips underscored continued deal activity in semiconductor infrastructure; while big names that include Nvidia, TSMC and ASML stayed in focus amid export-control discussions and ongoing investment in advanced manufacturing.

Hardware suppliers tied to cloud and AI infrastructure held their ground on solid demand signals, while some software and services firms lagged amid lingering growth headwinds.

The Technology Select Sector SPDR Fund (NYSEARCA:XLK), which tracks major U.S. tech stocks, slipped -0.23% this week, reflecting cautious sentiment among investors.

Let’s take a look at this week’s top gainers and losers in the tech industry:

Top weekly gainers:

ON Semiconductor (NASDAQ:ON) +14.84% gained this week amid optimism over its long-term growth outlook, aided by partnerships with Xiaomi in electric vehicles and Nvidia in data center power infrastructure.

KLA (NASDAQ:KLAC) +12.61% rose on strong margins and leadership in process control.

Micron Technology (NASDAQ:MU) +11.44% gained over the week as analysts turned upbeat on AI-related memory demand, with Citi projecting “unprecedented” DRAM growth and broader semiconductor sentiment lifted by target hikes from Cantor Fitzgerald.

Monolithic Power System (NASDAQ:MPWR) +11.08% rose after brokerage upgrades, citing strong revenue performance and growing market share in Nvidia’s Blackwell Ultra AI chips.

Western Digital (NASDAQ:WDC) +9.34% amid strong demand, tight supply, and improving pricing and margins expected to extend through 2026 and possibly 2027.

Top weekly losers:

F5 (NASDAQ:FFIV) –9.47% fell after the company disclosed a cyberattack by a suspected nation-state actor that accessed parts of its product development systems.

Arista Networks (NYSE:ANET) -7.14%

Hewlett Packard Enterprise (NYSE:HPE) -5.90% fell after its analyst meeting, as earnings guidance for next year missed expectations.

Datadog (NASDAQ:DDOG) -3.70%

Fair Issac (NYSE:FICO) -2.96% amid investor caution over regulatory and competitive pressures on its credit scoring and data analytics business.

U.S. Tech-based ETFs to track: (NASDAQ:QQQ), (NYSEARCA:VGT), (NYSEARCA:XLK), (NASDAQ:SMH), (NYSEARCA:IYW), (NYSEARCA:FTEC), (NASDAQ:SOXX), (BATS:IGV), (NASDAQ:CIBR), (NYSEARCA:IGM), (NYSEARCA:IXN).

data.title: Blackstone, TPG said to be nearing over $17B deal for Hologic
data.description:
• Private equity firms Blackstone (NYSE:BX) and TPG (NASDAQ:TPG) are in late-stage talks to acquire Hologic (NASDAQ:HOLX) in a transaction that could value the cancer test maker at more than $17B, including debt, Bloomberg News reported on Saturday, citing people familiar with the matter.
  • The companies are currently negotiating a price target of over $75 per share, the people said, adding that a deal could be announced in the coming days.
  • Shares of Hologic (NASDAQ:HOLX) closed higher on Thursday after Bloomberg News reported about the duo’s plans to acquire the Marlborough, Massachusetts-based medical device maker. The stock added another ~2% on Friday as takeover rumors continued.
  • A final agreement has yet to be reached, and the talks could still end prematurely, the people said.

data.title: Trending stocks this week: Wall Street ends higher despite trade and bank jitters
data.description:
It was a volatile week on Wall Street, but the three major indexes still ended with solid gains. Investors weighed persistent U.S.-China trade tensions, a prolonged government shutdown, the kickoff of the third-quarter earnings season, and renewed concerns about the health of regional banks.

For the week, the S&P (SP500) gained +1.7%, while the blue-chip Dow (DJI) added +1.6%. The tech-heavy Nasdaq Composite (COMP:IND) advanced +2.1%.

As the market swayed to the events of this week, these were among the top trending stocks:

Quarterly results from major banks took the spotlight this week. The biggest upside was seen by Wells Fargo (WFC), which finished Tuesday's trading higher by 7% after beating expectations with its Q3 earnings. Morgan Stanley (MS) was another standout among financial earnings, with the stock climbing 5% after Q3 earnings posted a strong beat, bolstered by robust investment banking and equities activity.

Loan defaults at two regional banks fueled worries and triggered a slump in the stock market. Zions Bancorporation (ZION) dropped 13% on Thursday after reporting a charge-off on a loan issued by a subsidiary, while Western Alliance Bancorp (WAL) fell 11% after disclosing loans to the same borrowers. Investors are also watching the private credit sector closely following the failures of subprime auto lender Tricolor Holdings and auto parts supplier First Brands Group. Major banks, including JPMorgan Chase & Co. (JPM) and Jefferies Financial Group (JEF), reported losses linked to these cases.

Broadcom (AVGO) shares rose after the company announced a partnership with OpenAI (OPENAI) to build 10 gigawatts of custom AI accelerators. OpenAI will design the chips and systems, while Broadcom will oversee their development and deployment.

AST SpaceMobile (ASTS) fell as Barclays downgraded the firm to an investment rating of "underweight" from "overweight". Analyst Mathieu Robilliard justified the move by saying that the stock is currently overvalued.

Micron (MU) fell amid a Reuters report that the chipmaker plans to stop supplying server chips to data centers in China after the business failed to recover from a 2023 government ban on its products in critical Chinese infrastructure.

Bloom Energy (BE) rose after announcing a $5 billion strategic partnership with Brookfield Asset Management (BAM) to become the preferred onsite power provider for Brookfield's global artificial intelligence factories.

Astera Labs (ALAB) plunged following news of AMD's (AMD) new partnership with Oracle (ORCL) to deploy 50,000 GPUs beginning in the second half of 2026.

Oracle (ORCL) said at its Financial Analyst Day on Thursday that it expects AI infrastructure projects to deliver gross margins of 30%–40%. The company also projected annual earnings per share of $21 by fiscal 2030. Oracle (ORCL) also hosted an AI World event in Las Vegas this week, where it revealed its new AI Data Platform, agents, and a wide array of new partnerships.

Papa John's (PZZA) jumped after a report that Apollo Global (APO) made a $64 a share bid for the pizza chain, according to a StreetInsider report.

Nebius Group (NBIS) introduced its latest AI cloud - Nebius AI Cloud 3.0 “Aether” - delivering enterprise-grade features that give organizations the trust, control, and simplicity they need to run their most critical AI workloads in production and at scale. However, shares fell over 16% in the week.

data.title: Astra, Daiichi post late-stage data to show Enhertu’s potential in early breast cancer
data.description:
AstraZeneca (NASDAQ:AZN) and partner Daiichi Sankyo (OTCPK:DSKYF) (OTCPK:DSNKY) on Saturday announced data from two late-stage trials to indicate how their blockbuster antitumor agent Enhertu could be effective in early breast cancer.

The antibody-drug conjugate is currently indicated in the U.S. to treat unresectable or metastatic breast cancer.

Citing data from their DESTINY-Breast05 and DESTINY-Breast11 Phase 3 trials as presented at a European medical event, the companies highlighted Enhertus' potential to cure more patients by becoming a foundational therapy in early-stage breast cancer.

According to data from DESTINY-Breast05, Enhertu, as part of a combination regimen, improved the invasive disease-free survival (IDFS) in patients with HER2-positive early breast cancer who had a high risk of disease recurrence.

The open-label trial of over 1,500 subjects evaluated the intravenous therapy against trastuzumab emtansine (T-DM1), another antibody drug conjugate marketed by Roche (OTCQX:RHHBY) as Kadcyla in a post-neo-adjuvant setup in patients with residual invasive disease.

Citing a July 2 data cut, the duo said Enhertu cut the risk of invasive disease recurrence or death by a statistically significant 53% compared to T-DM1, and the drug indicated a safety profile in line with its established tolerability profile with no new safety signals.

Data from DESTINY-Breast11 showed that Enhertu, followed by the cancer therapy combo THP, caused a statistically significant and clinically meaningful improvement in the pathologic complete response ((pCR)) rate.

The global trial of more than 900 patients was designed to evaluate neoadjuvant Enhertu followed by THP against ddAC-THP in high-risk HER2-positive early-stage breast cancer.

According to a data cut on Mar. 12, Enhertu, followed by THP, led to a pCR improvement of 11.2% with a statistically significant effect and no new safety signals. The companies added that there was a favorable trend in terms of event-free survival, a secondary endpoint, but the data were immature at the time of the analysis.

IDFS and pCR rates were the primary endpoints of DESTINY-Breast05 and DESTINY-Breast11, respectively. Data for overall survival (OS), a key secondary endpoint in DESTINY-Breast05, were not mature at the time of the analysis.

data.title: Communication services weekly roundup: Alphabet leads, The Trade Desk lags
data.description:
U.S. stocks wavered amid increased volatility as the U.S.-China trade war increased uncertainity, while the U.S. government shutdown entered its 17th day.
 
 
Overall, the sector, represented by the Communication Services Select Sector SPDR ETF (NYSEARCA:XLC), gained 1% in the week, while the broader market (S&P 500) added 1.7%.
 
 

Weekly top gainers

  • Alphabet (GOOGL) surged 7.07% and (GOOG) 6.86% in the week.
  • Warner Bros. Discovery (WBD) moved up by 6.37%. WBD rose on report that a bid from Paramount Skydance (PSKY) may come as soon as this week.
  • AppLovin (APP) gained 5.16%.
  • Take-Two Interactive Software (TTWO) advanced 3.72%.

Biggest weekly decliners

  • The Trade Desk (TTD) fell 4.55% in the week.
  • Charter Communications (CHTR) slipped 2.44%.
  • Netflix (NFLX) dropped 1.70%.
  • Paramount Skydance (PSKY) shares lost 1.35%.
 

ETFs to tab the communication services sector include: (NYSEARCA:XLC), (VOX), (IYZ), (RSPC), and (XTL).

data.title: Weekly consumer staples check: Bungee Global and Estee Lauder tops chart
data.description:
Wall Street stocks wavered amid increased volatility on Friday but managed to resurge, as Wall Street was rattled by concerns on the health of regional banks and credit risks while the U.S. government shutdown continued.

Let us look at last week's best and worst performers in the consumer staples segment.

Top weekly gainers include:

  • Bungee Global (BG) +20.71% despite the company revising its 2025 adjusted EPS outlook to $7.30–$7.60, down from the prior estimate of ~$7.75, to reflect the Viterra integration and macro conditions. The company revised its segment and volume reporting structure to align with its value chain operating model following the merger with Viterra.
  • Estee Lauder (EL) +14.98% after Goldman Sachs upgrades to Buy from Hold, citing a turning corner and possible return to topline growth as soon as Q1 with double-digit EBIT margins by FY27.
  • Dollar Tree (DLTR) +10.08% reaffirmed its near-term guidance ahead of the retailer's Investor Day event. The company also noted that comparable same-store sales growth is up 3.8% so far in FQ3.
  • Keurig Dr Pepper (KDP) +8.56% after report that prominent  activist Starboard Value has taken a stake after its announced €15.7 billion deal for JDE Peet's was received poorly by investors. of 
  • Brown-Forman (BF.B) +7.53% CEO Lawson Whiting discussed the company’s ambition to double its size while also acknowledging that softer global spirits demand and external economic challenges will push back their timeline by one or two years beyond the original target date of 2032 target.

Top weekly losers include:  

  • Kenvue (KVUE) -8.17% after reports of claimants seeking damages from J&J (NYSE:JNJ) and Kenvue UK Limited, a unit of Kenvue (NYSE:KVUE), the consumer care spinoff of the New Brunswick, New Jersey-based pharma giant.
  • Hershey Company (HSY) -2.28%
  • Altria Group (MO) -2.24%
  • General Mills (GIS) -1.93%
  • Philip Morris (PM) -1.50%

ETFs linking to consumer staples: (NYSEARCA:VDC), (NYSEARCA:FXG), (NYSEARCA:IYK), (NYSEARCA:KXI), (NYSEARCA:FSTA), (NASDAQ:PSL).

data.title: Novo Nordisk GLP-1 pill now U.S. approved for heart disease risk
data.description:
Novo Nordisk (NVO) has received approval to market its oral GLP-1 medicine Rybelsus in the U.S. to reduce the risk of major cardiovascular events such as stroke in adults with type 2 diabetes, the Danish drugmaker announced.

Having first approved the pill in 2019 for use with diet and exercise to treat type 2 diabetes in adults, the FDA expanded its label to include patients at a higher risk for major adverse cardiovascular events, the company announced late Friday.

The approval marks the first time an oral GLP-1 medicine will be indicated in the U.S. to cut the risk of MACE events in this patient group, Novo (NVO) added.

The decision is based on data from the company’s Phase 3b SOUL trial, in which the once-daily therapy at a 14 mg dose led to a statistically significant 14% reduction in risk of MACE events compared to placebo at four years.

In 2024, the FDA approved Novo’s (OTCPK:NONOF) obesity therapy Wegovy to cut the risk of MACE events in obese or overweight adults with established heart disease. An oral form of the popular injectable is currently undergoing FDA review for the treatment of obesity, with a decision expected by the end of this year.

data.title: AGNC stock stands to benefit from tightening in spreads despite lower estimated Q3 earnings
data.description:
A bellwether for mortgage REITs, AGNC Investment (NASDAQ:AGNC), is scheduled to announce Q3 financial results on Monday, October 20th, before market open, and the stock stands to benefit from the recent tightening in spreads despite lower expected earnings.

The consensus EPS estimate is $0.39 (-9.3% Y/Y).

Over the last 2 years, AGNC has beaten EPS estimates 50% of the time. Over the last 3 months, EPS estimates have seen 1 upward revision and 7 downward.

Last quarter, earnings fell short of the average analyst estimate as tariff news roiled markets in the spring. While most markets bounced back, agency mortgage-backed securities did not.

"Although most asset class valuations retraced the April losses and ultimately increased quarter-over-quarter, agency MBS were an exception, as spreads to benchmark rates widened moderately during the second quarter. As a result of this underperformance, AGNC's economic return for the second quarter was -1.0%," said CEO Peter Federico.

"Looking ahead, we expect banks and foreign demand for agency MBS to grow. In addition, as we enter the third quarter, the seasonal supply pattern for MBS issuance should improve. We expect the net supply of new MBS will be about $200 billion this year, the low end of most forecasts. Since quarter end, MBS spreads have tightened slightly and are showing signs of stabilization," Federico had said during the second-quarter earnings call.

"We have started to see a positive shift in our mortgage universe with 30-year mortgage rates decreasing to 6.3% from 7%+ earlier in the year and agency spreads tightening materially," said Piper Sandler analyst Crispin Love in the Q3 mortgage finance preview.

"We expect key topics on the (NASDAQ:AGNC) earnings call to be on spreads, mortgage rates, investment opportunities, and GSE reform. Going forward, we believe AGNC can maintain its current dividend level, with AGNC generating mid-to-high teens returns," noted Love in the research note.

"We are maintaining our 3Q25 core EPS estimate of $0.38 and our 2025 and 2026 estimates of $1.57 and $1.54, respectively. However, we are increasing our 3Q25 TBV (tangible book value) estimate to $8.60 from $7.94 as agency spreads have tightened ~20 bps from mid-July to quarter end. We are also increasing our GAAP EPS estimate to $1.12 from $0.45," said Love.

Piper Sandler raised its price target on the stock to $10.50 from $10.00, citing the action to be driven by higher tangible book value given recent spread tightening.

Ahead of the Q3 earnings release, the average Wall Street analysts' rating on the stock stands at Buy.

On the other side, Seeking Alpha authors see the stock as Hold.

"AGNC aggressively bought up swaps when interest rates were super low. Those swaps have been maturing, and we can see AGNC's cost of funds drift upward," noted SA contributor Rida Morwa.

"It is important to note that through the end of 2025, the swaps expiring are going to have a larger financial impact than repo rates coming down," said Morwa.

"For traders who buy the rumor and sell the news, AGNC is going to move quickly into the category of no longer meeting any of their interests. This year, AGNC has strongly outperformed the market on the expectation of interest rate cuts, not that they're here, we're seeing traders exit their positions, bringing current returns closer to that of the overall market," noted Morwa.

"For income investors who position their portfolio to benefit regardless of what interest rates are doing, AGNC continues to be a highly attractive opportunity to collect double-digit yields paid out monthly from ultra-safe investments in agency MBS," according to the author.

The stock has added ~8.5% year-to-date, and closed 0.71% higher at $9.99 on Friday ahead of its Q3 earnings release.

data.title: Bond king Bill Gross warns about regional bank “cockroaches” and gold’s momentum rally
data.description:
Bond-market legend Bill Gross has warned about the recent rally in gold prices and argued that credit risk concerns that have caused a selloff among regional banks will continue to impact stocks and bonds.

His comments came after gold (XAUUSD:CUR) and silver (XAGUSD:CUR) prices reached new all-time highs early this week before sliding on Friday as U.S.-China trade tensions eased, with President Donald Trump noting that a "full-scale" tariff on Chinese imports would be unsustainable.

“Gold has become a momentum/meme asset. If you want to own it, wait a while,” the Pimco founder said in an X post on Friday as Gold Trust ETF (NYSEARCA:GLD) recorded its first intraday drop after five straight sessions of gains and a YTD rally of ~61%.

Meanwhile, the NYSE Arca Gold Miners Index (GDX) headed to its biggest intraday drop since May with a ~7% decline, with Newmont (NEM), Barrick (B), and Agnico Eagle Mines (AEM) among notable decliners.

“Regional bank 'cockroaches' may continue to affect stocks AND bonds,” Gross said regarding the financial sector, referring to a term JPMorgan Chase (JPM) CEO Jamie Dimon used earlier this week to identify the credit risk caused by the bankruptcy of car parts supplier First Brands Group.

“I probably shouldn’t say this, but when you see one cockroach, there are probably more,” Dimon said during the company’s Q3 earnings call on Tuesday, adding, “And so we should—everyone should be forewarned on this one.”

SPDR S&P Regional Banking ETF (NYSEARCA:KRE) fell ~6% on Thursday, driven by notable decliners such as Jefferies Financial (JEF), Zions Bancorporation (ZION), and Western Alliance (WAL), which also flagged credit losses.

However, the sector recovered some losses on Friday with largely positive commentary from banks, and as analysts pointed out, the industry appears to be on a solid footing, and credit risk events could not indicate a systemic issue.
